2604.16989.title: Bolzano: Case Studies in LLM-Assisted Mathematical Research
2604.16989.paper_summary: We report new results on six problems in mathematics and theoretical computer science, produced with the assistance of Bolzano, an open-source multi-agent LLM system. Bolzano orchestrates rounds of interaction between parallel prover agents and a verifier agent while maintaining a persistent knowledge base that is carried across rounds. Classified using the significance-autonomy taxonomy of Feng et al., four of the six results reach the level of publishable research, and three of the six were produced essentially autonomously by Bolzano. Our results provide evidence that LLMs can contribute meaningfully to mathematical research, complementing recent reports by Bubeck et al., Woodruff et al., and others.
2604.16989.paper_summary_zh: 我們報告了六個數學和理論計算機科學問題的新結果，這些結果是在開源多代理 LLM 系統 Bolzano 的協助下產生的。Bolzano 協調平行證明代理和驗證代理之間的互動回合，同時維持一個持久的知識庫，該知識庫在回合之間保持不變。根據 Feng 等人的重要性-自主性分類法，六個結果中有四個達到了可發表研究的水平，而六個結果中有三個基本上是由 Bolzano 自主產生的。我們的結果提供了證據，表明 LLM 可以對數學研究做出有意義的貢獻，這補充了 Bubeck 等人、Woodruff 等人及其他人的近期報告。
2604.16989.author: Jan Grebík et.al.
2604.16989.authors: Jan Grebík, Pavel Hubáček, Martin Koutecký, Matěj Kripner, Václav Rozhoň, Robert Šámal, Adrián Zámečník
